entries.thesis: ⭐ INTC +3.4% · INTC is up 3.44% on the day and 13.17%…
Pure-play NAND supplier (post-WDC spin); same pricing tailwind as Micron with even higher operating leverage on NAND ASPs.
Dominant supplier of NAND/DRAM etch & deposition equipment; sustained memory tightness pulls forward memory-maker capex, of which Lam captures the largest share.
Major memory wafer fab equipment supplier; benefits from same memory capex pull-forward, though less memory-mix than Lam.
Server vendor with heavy DRAM content per unit; AI/enterprise server gross margins compressed by memory inflation.
Ion implant exposure to memory fabs; smaller-cap leveraged play on memory capex cycle re-acceleration.
Consumer electronics peripherals maker; memory in webcams/peripherals plus weaker consumer wallet share if Apple hikes prices and absorbs consumer spend.
Consumer device maker using NAND/DRAM in wearables/auto; smaller scale means worse memory contract pricing than Apple, hitting margins.
Pure-play US memory maker (DRAM + NAND); Apple publicly confirming a structural supply shortage validates higher contract pricing and ASPs directly flowing to Micron's revenue/margins.
Server and PC OEM with massive DRAM/NAND BOM exposure; AI server margins already thin, and memory is the single largest cost line — pass-through is harder than for Apple.
PC OEM with low single-digit margins; memory cost spike is a direct gross-margin hit and PC demand is price-elastic, limiting pass-through.
AI server assembler running on thin margins; every GPU server ships with terabytes of DRAM and NVMe — memory spike directly compresses already-pressured margins.
HDD-heavy post-spin but still benefits as enterprise storage demand shifts to HDDs when NAND tightens/prices spike.

Most direct pure-play AI server peer; Dell's 750%+ AI server growth confirms the end-market is expanding fast enough for both, easing fears SMCI is losing share to Dell.
AI server clusters require high-speed Ethernet switching; Dell's AI growth implies more back-end network buildouts where Arista is winning share vs InfiniBand.
Direct enterprise server competitor; Dell's 750% AI server growth implies HPE is losing AI server share, especially after HPE's softer AI server commentary and the Juniper integration distraction.
Competes for enterprise AI infrastructure wallet share; Dell capturing enterprise AI server budgets pressures IBM's Power/zSystems and hybrid AI positioning.
Dell AI servers are overwhelmingly Hopper/Blackwell-based; 750% revenue growth is a direct demand signal for NVDA GPU shipments into the enterprise/sovereign AI channel.
Vertiv supplies thermal management and power distribution into Dell/SMCI AI server racks; accelerating AI rack shipments drive content-per-rack for Vertiv.
Celestica's HPS segment ODMs AI server hardware for hyperscalers; Dell data point validates the broader AI server TAM that CLS is levered to.
Each AI server consumes multi-TB of HBM3e and high-capacity DDR5; Dell's volume ramp tightens already-constrained HBM/DDR5 demand.
